sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
OK so:
- Before LEFT: dark gray at cols 12-26, player at cols 42-56
- After LEFT: dark gray at cols 15-29, player at cols 39-53
LEFT moved the dark gray RIGHT by 3 columns (12→15, 26→29) and the player LEFT by 3 columns (42→39, 56→53).
So LEFT moves the dark gray rightward and the player leftward (toward each other).
And ACTION7 moves the dark gray leftward and the player rightward (away from each other).
This makes sense! LEFT brings them together, ACTION7 separates them.
So the dark gray and player are at the same vertical position (rows 18-29). The goal is probably to overlap them.
Currently:
- Dark gray: cols 15-29
- Player: cols 39-53
They're separated by cols 30-38 (9 columns). To overlap them, I need to move the dark gray rightward by about 10 columns (from 15-29 to 25-39 would overlap with player at 39).
Since LEFT moves the dark gray right by 3 columns, I need about 3-4 LEFTs to overlap them.
Let me try 3 LEFTs in a row.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Dark gray at cols 15-29, player at cols 39-53
# LEFT moves dark gray right by 3, player left by 3
# Need to close the gap of 10 columns (29 to 39)
# 3 LEFTs should move dark gray to 24-38, player to 30-47
# This would overlap at cols 30-38
action(['LEFT', 'LEFT', 'LEFT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
